question_text: A 50 kg student runs up a flight of stairs, gaining 10 meters of height in 5 seconds. The chemical energy required for this activity comes from food. Explain why the student requires more chemical energy from food than the gravitational potential energy gained by their body.
correct_answer: The human body is not 100% efficient. Much of the chemical energy from food is lost as heat during muscle contractions and metabolic processes. Only a fraction of the chemical energy is converted into useful gravitational potential energy. Human metabolism is approximately 20-25% efficient, meaning most energy is wasted as heat rather than used for work.
explanation: When your muscles contract to move you up the stairs, they convert chemical energy from food into motion and gravitational potential energy. However, muscles are not perfectly efficient machines. A lot of the chemical energy is wasted as heat through friction in the muscles and other biological processes. This is why you feel warm when exercising. Only about 20-25% of the chemical energy actually does useful work; the rest becomes heat.
